title: AI Builders Digest
subtitle: Bilingual edition · 双语对照版

intro.text: 今天内容有一条很清晰的主线：AI 工具的能力边界正在被快速推进。OpenAI 的 Thibault 说 GPT-5.5 是他们做过的最好模型，同时 token 效率提升带来了实际速度提升；Steipete 则在说 AI coding agent 已经能处理 4-10 小时的长任务，而且置信度比以前高很多；Vercel 的 Rauch 把 AI Gateway 做成了企业级产品；Anthropic 的 Claude 博客则推出了[做梦]功能，让 agent 能在会话之间自我改进。这些信号放在一起，说的都是同一件事：基础设施正在就绪，接下来的竞争在应用层。

archive.title: 基础设施就绪日报
archive.desc: GPT-5.5 速度提升、coding agent 跑 10 小时任务、AI Gateway 企业化、Claude 学会做梦——AI 基础设施正在快速就绪，竞争正在移向应用层。

footerNote: Source: Follow Builders curated daily digest. Rebuilt from the 2026-05-29 source feeds.

sections.title: GPT-5.5: The Speed and Quality Jump / GPT-5.5：速度与质量的同步提升
sections.desc: OpenAI 的 Thibault Sottiaux 说 GPT-5.5 是一次 token 效率的突破，这不仅是能力提升，更是实际产品速度的改善。

rewrite: Thibault Sottiaux breaks down what the GPT-5.0 to GPT-5.5 increment actually means: each number bump comes with capability improvements and token efficiency gains that translate to real speed in production.
He confirms GPT-5.5 is their best model yet—a simple strategy they intend to keep pushing forward.
The 3842 likes suggests the developer community is tracking these model improvements closely, not just for capability benchmarks but for practical inference cost implications.
original: When we go from GPT-5.0 -> GPT-5.1 -> ... -> GPT-5.5, the number incrementing goes with improvements in capabilities and token efficiency (which translates to speed gains). With GPT-5.5 our best model yet. A simple strategy that we would like to continue.

rewrite: Thibault Sottiaux 拆解了 GPT-5.0 到 GPT-5.5 的数字增量实际意味着什么：每次数字上涨都伴随着能力提升和 token 效率改进，这些最终转化为实际产品速度的提升。
他确认 GPT-5.5 是他们做过的最好模型——他们打算继续这个简单的策略。
3842 个赞说明开发者社区不仅在关注模型能力 benchmark，更在关注实际的推理成本影响。
original: GPT-5.0 到 GPT-5.5 的数字增长伴随着能力提升和 token 效率改进（意味着速度提升）。GPT-5.5 是他们做过的最好模型。简单策略，会继续。

sections.title: AI Agents Are Getting Serious / AI Agent 正在变得严肃起来
sections.desc: Steipete 的数据：AI coding agent 已经能处理 4-10 小时的长任务，置信度比以前高很多；Dan Shipper 则用数据说话：41 天 streak、38b tokens、最长单任务 56 小时。

rewrite: Peter Steinberger quantifies the productivity shift with concrete numbers: with GPT-5.5 and /goal plus autoreview, prompts that used to take 30-60 minutes now complete in 4-10 hours, with much higher confidence that the result is ready.
His conclusion: yielding agents is a skill. This isn't just about tools getting better—it's about how you work with them.
The 2905 likes confirm this lands for builders who are deep in the daily loop.
original: With GPT 5.5, /goal, autoreview and crabbox my prompts moved from ~30-60min to often 4-10h tasks and my confidence that it's ready is much much higher. Yielding agents is a skill.

rewrite: Peter Steinberger 用具体数字量化了生产力转变：有了 GPT-5.5 和 /goal 加 autoreview 之后，以前 30-60 分钟的任务现在经常 4-10 小时完成，而且对结果已经ready的置信度比以前高很多。
他的结论：yielding agents 是一种技能。这不只是工具变好了的问题——是如何跟它们配合的问题。
2905 个赞说明这击中了那些天天泡在里面工作的 builders。
original: 用 GPT-5.5、/goal、autoreview、crabbox，任务从 30-60 分钟变成 4-10 小时，置信度高很多。Yielding agents 是一种技能。

rewrite: Steinberger shares a debugging pattern for Codex: tell it there is a bug, and it will loop and loop until it finds the issue. The signal: agents need directional hints to focus their search, not just open-ended prompts.
This is a practical complement to his productivity tweet—same person, two sides of the same coin: how to work with agents effectively.
original: I do this with codex all the time. Ask it to review code for bugs and it will tell you all good, tell it there is a bug and it will LOOP AND LOOP and will find issues.

rewrite: Steinberger 分享了一个 Codex 调试模式：告诉它这里有 bug，它就会一直循环直到找到问题。信号是：agent 需要方向性提示来聚焦搜索，不是开放式 prompt。
这是他那条生产力推文的实际补充——同一个人，同一枚硬币的两面：如何有效地跟 agent 配合工作。
original: 用 Codex 调试：让它 review 代码说一切正常，它就不动；告诉它有 bug，它就会循环查找直到找到问题。

rewrite: Dan Shipper runs the numbers on his Codex usage: 38 billion tokens processed, 41-day current streak, and a 56-hour longest task. His conclusion: lfg codex.
These metrics—token volume, streak length, task duration—are becoming the new way builders quantify their AI collaboration intensity, replacing vague claims about 'being more productive.'
original: 38b tokens and a 56h longest task. 41 day current streak. lfg codex.

rewrite: Dan Shipper 报了他的 Codex 使用数据：380 亿 tokens、当前连续 41 天、最长单任务 56 小时。他的结论：lfg codex。
这些指标——token 量、连续天数、任务时长——正在变成 builders 量化 AI 协作强度的新方式，取代「更高效了」这种模糊说法。
original: 380 亿 tokens，56 小时最长任务，41 天连续 streak。lfg codex。

sections.title: Enterprise AI Goes Real / 企业 AI 走向实操
sections.desc: Box CEO Aaron Levie 用数据说明 AI 实际上在帮企业招人而不是裁员，Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch 则推出了 AI Gateway 的 per-API-key 消费上限功能。

rewrite: Aaron Levie brings field data from enterprise conversations: outside Silicon Valley, a meaningful number of companies are hiring while also adopting AI—not in spite of it. The freed-up dollars go back into the business, into sales, marketing, customer success.

He references Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon's NYTimes OpEd as the clearest articulation of this pattern. The static-world view—automation replaces jobs—is wrong. Companies don't remain static. They reinvest.
This is the most grounded enterprise take in the jobs debate right now.
original: In the majority of conversations I have with CIOs, CTOs, and CEOs in large enterprises, they are either growing due to AI (in new job functions like FDEs, engineering, etc.) or at a minimum reinvesting efficiency savings back into the business in new areas (sales, marketing, etc.). Most businesses have been constrained by how much software they can produce... When AI makes it possible to do more of this, investment goes back into the business.

rewrite: Aaron Levie 带来了一线企业对话中的数据：硅谷以外，很多公司都在一边招人一边用 AI——不是因为 AI 减少了岗位，而是因为 AI 释放出来的资金被重新投入业务，用于销售、市场、客户服务。
他引用 Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon 在《纽约时报》专栏的文章作为这个模式最清晰的表述。静态世界观的观点——自动化等于岗位消失——是错的。公司不会静止不动，它们在重新投资。
这是目前岗位争论中最接地气的企业视角。
original: Levie 说大多数企业的 CIO/CTO/CEO 在用 AI 的同时都在招人（或把省下来的钱投入新领域）。这是 Goldman Sachs CEO 也说过的话。公司不是静止的，省下的钱会重新投入。

rewrite: Guillermo Rauch announces per-API-key spend caps on AI Gateway—a practical enterprise control that lets teams set hard limits on AI spending per key, preventing runaway costs at the operational level.
The framing matters: after the initial AI excitement, enterprise buyers want operational controls and predictability, not just capability increments.
original: Per-API Key spend caps on AI Gateway.

rewrite: Guillermo Rauch 宣布 AI Gateway 支持 per-API-key 消费上限——这是一个实用的企业级控制功能，让团队能为每个 key 设置硬性消费上限，防止成本失控。
这个表述很重要：在最初的 AI 热潮之后，企业买家想要的是运营控制和可预测性，不只是能力数字的增长。
original: Vercel AI Gateway 支持 per-API-key 消费上限。

rewrite: Rauch's brief command to founders cuts through the AI hype: ship the best product. Use lots of AI, some AI, maybe no AI. Just be the best. No checklist required.
This is the anti-hype frame that resonates with builders who've been through the SaaS era and remember that the best product usually wins, regardless of which features are on the slide.
original: Ship the best product. Use lots of AI, some AI, maybe no AI. Just be the best.

rewrite: Rauch 对 founders 的简短指令切中了 AI 炒作的要害：做出最好的产品。用很多 AI，用一点 AI，可能不用 AI。只要做到最好就行。不需要清单。
这是反炒作的框架，跟那些经历过 SaaS 时代、记得「最好的产品通常会赢」的 builders 产生了共鸣，不管 PPT 上有哪些功能标签。
original: 做出最好的产品。用很多 AI、一点 AI、可能不用 AI。只要做到最好就行。

sections.title: Cursor & Design Tools / Cursor 和设计工具
sections.desc: Ryo Lu 展示了 Cursor 的 auto-review 如何帮助新 coder 学习，Zara Zhang 则注意到 Claude Opus 4.8 在写作风格上的微妙变化。

rewrite: Ryo Lu (Design at Cursor) shares what he loves about auto-review: Cursor explains the command and the risk, which makes it much easier for new coders to learn and actually do things. The implication: AI code tools are becoming onboarding infrastructure for the next generation of builders.
The product insight is sharp: learning and doing are the same action when the tool explains itself.
original: What I love about auto-review: Cursor explains the command and risk, makes it much easier for new coders to learn and just do things.

rewrite: Ryo Lu（Cursor Design）分享了他喜欢 auto-review 的地方：Cursor 会解释这个命令是什么、有什么风险，这让新 coder 学起来容易很多，也更容易真的动手做事。含义是：AI 编程工具正在变成下一代 builder 的入职基础设施。
产品洞察很锋利：当工具能解释自己的时候，学习和做事是同一件事。
original: 喜欢 auto-review 的地方：Cursor 解释命令和风险，让新 coder 更轻松地学习和做事。

rewrite: Zara Zhang noticed a specific stylistic shift in Opus 4.8: it stopped using em dashes in writing. Whether this is a deliberate style choice or an emergent behavior, it shows how closely the builder community is watching model output at a granular level.
original: One thing I noticed about Opus 4.8 is that it stopped using em dashes in writing.

rewrite: Zara Zhang 注意到 Opus 4.8 的一个具体风格变化：它在写作中停止使用 em dash。无论这是有意的风格选择还是涌现行为，它都说明 builder 群体正在极其细致地观察模型输出的每个细节。
original: Opus 4.8 停止在写作中使用 em dash 了。

sections.title: Startup Patterns / 创业模式观察
sections.desc: Garry Tan 在号召加州投票，Nikunj 继续他「手里握着外星超级智能还想用老方法」的论调，Aditya Agarwal 则从印度生育数据谈到了城市的育儿困境。

rewrite: Garry Tan is pushing his California voting guide at garrysguide.org ahead of the June 2 deadline—the practical version of his week-long civic push. For builders paying attention to Bay Area policy, this is the actionable item.
original: Don't forget to vote by June 2. Use my guide to see how we can fix California. garrysguide dot org.

rewrite: Garry Tan 在 6 月 2 日截止日前大力推他的加州投票指南 garrysguide.org——这是他整周公民推动的行动版本。对关注 Bay Area 政策的 builders 来说，这是可以立刻行动的项目。
original: 6 月 2 日前记得投票。用我的指南了解如何修复加州。garrysguide.org。

rewrite: Nikunj shares what might be Grok gassing him up, but says it makes him unreasonably happy—the soccer emoji captures the vibe. builders sharing their irrational enthusiasm is a different kind of signal than the usual polished takes.
original: This might be just Grok's way of gassing me up - but it makes me unreasonably happy.

rewrite: Nikunj 分享了可能是 Grok 在给他拍马屁的内容，但他说这让他「不合理地开心」——那个足球 emoji 捕捉到了这种氛围。Builders 分享自己非理性的热情，这是一种跟平时那些打磨过的观点不同的信号。
original: 可能是 Grok 在拍马屁，但让我不合理地开心。

rewrite: Aditya Agarwal shares personal context about India's low birth rate: most people he talks to have 1 child, some have 2. When he says he has three, the reaction is surprise followed by 'well, accidents happen.' The main reason for one child: raising kids is hard, especially for nuclear families in cities.
This is personal context, not a tech post—but it lands in the digest because it humanizes the demographic data everyone quotes.
original: Most people I talk to in India have 1 child. Some have 2. When I tell them I have three, their first reaction is total surprise followed by 'well, accidents happen'. The main reason for one? Raising kids is just really hard, especially for nuclear families in cities.

rewrite: Aditya Agarwal 分享了关于印度低生育率的个人背景：他交谈的大多数印度人只有 1 个孩子，有些有 2 个。当他说自己有 3 个时，反应先是惊讶，然后是「好吧，意外总会发生的」。只生一个的主要原因：养孩子真的很难，特别是在城市里的核心家庭。
这是个人背景，不是科技帖——但它进入 digest 是因为它让每个人都在引用的生育率数据有了人的质感。
original: 在印度跟人聊，他们大多只有 1 个孩子，有些 2 个。我说有 3 个，反应先是惊讶然后说「好吧意外总会发生」。只生一个的主要原因：养孩子很难，尤其城市核心家庭。

sections.title: Claude Managed Agents Update / Claude Managed Agents 更新
sections.desc: Anthropic 推出了 Claude Managed Agents 的「做梦」功能预览，以及 outcomes、multiagent orchestration 和 webhooks——让 agent 能在会话之间自我改进。

rewrite: Oriol Vinyals, co-lead of Gemini alongside Jeff Dean, sits down after Google I/O to cover the full arc of what's coming: multimodal world models, memory and reasoning as the next frontier, and the role of scaffolding in making these systems actually usable.

His framing on world models is the headline: the model needs to understand not just text or images, but the structure of the physical and digital world to reason about consequences. This is what separates today's impressive demos from systems that actually plan and act.

On memory: Vinyals describes it as the key unlock for the next few years—models that remember what they learned across sessions and can apply it. The current context window approach has limits; persistent memory changes what 'learning' means for a model.
The conversation is dense with specifics from someone shaping the frontier, not marketing speak.
original: The conversation with Oriol Vinyals covers: what's required for further advances in multimodal models, world models and their usability, the increase in memory and how advances there will drive reasoning in the next few years, the state of scaffolding today and what persists, and how these systems will evolve.

rewrite: Gemini 联合负责人 Oriol Vinyals 和 Jeff Dean 一起，在 Google I/O 之后坐下来，覆盖了即将发生的事情的完整脉络：多模态世界模型、记忆和推理作为下一个前沿、以及 scaffolding 在让这些系统真正可用方面的作用。
他关于世界模型的表述是头条：模型需要的不仅仅是理解文本或图像，而是要理解物理世界和数字世界的结构，才能推理后果。这区分了今天那些令人印象深刻的 demo 和那些真正能计划和行动的系统。
关于记忆：Vinyals 将其描述为未来几年的关键解锁——能记住跨会话学到的内容并能应用的模型。当前的 context 窗口方法有局限；持久记忆改变了「学习」对模型意味着什么。
这场对话充满了来自塑造前沿的人的具体信息，不是营销语言。
original: 与 Oriol Vinyals 的对话涵盖：多模态模型进一步进步需要什么、世界模型及其可用性、记忆的增加及其如何推动未来几年的推理、当下 scaffolding 的状态及其持续性、以及这些系统将如何演进。

rewrite: Anthropic's Claude Managed Agents launches three major updates: 'dreaming' as a research preview, outcomes-based evaluation, and multiagent orchestration with webhooks.

Dreaming is the most conceptually interesting: a scheduled process that reviews your agent sessions and memory stores, extracts patterns, and curates memories so agents improve over time without direct human intervention. You choose how much control—automatic or reviewed before landing.

The outcomes feature lets you write a rubric describing what success looks like, and a separate grader evaluates the output in its own context window—so the evaluation isn't influenced by the agent's reasoning. Agents iterate until the grader passes.

Multiagent orchestration and webhooks complete the picture: complex workflows where multiple specialized agents coordinate, with external systems triggering events. This is infrastructure for agentic products, not just experiments.
original: Today we're launching dreaming in Claude Managed Agents as a research preview. Dreaming extends memory by reviewing past sessions to find patterns and help agents self-improve. We're also making outcomes, multiagent orchestration, and webhooks available to developers building with Managed Agents.

rewrite: Anthropic 的 Claude Managed Agents 推出三大更新：「做梦」作为研究预览、基于 outcomes 的评估、以及多 agent 编排和 webhooks。
「做梦」是最具概念趣味的：一个定期执行的过程，审查你的 agent 会话和记忆库，提取模式，整理记忆，让 agent 在没有直接人工干预的情况下随着时间改进。你选择有多少控制权——自动应用还是经过审查再落地。
Outcomes 功能让你写一个描述成功标准的评分标准，一个独立的 grader 在自己的 context 窗口里评估输出——所以评估不会受到 agent 推理的影响。Agent 迭代直到 grader 通过。
多 agent 编排和 webhooks 完善了这个图景：多个专精 agent 协调的复杂工作流，外部系统触发事件。这是 agentic 产品的基础设施，不只是实验。
original: 今天推出「做梦」作为研究预览，还有 outcomes、多 agent 编排和 webhooks。做梦是定期审查会话和记忆、提取模式、让 agent 自我改进的机制。Outcomes 让你写成功标准，由独立 grader 评估。多 agent 编排支持复杂工作流。
